name: The Rich Fool's Barns
oracleText:
At the beginning of your end step, if you control fewer than three Treasures, create a Treasure token.
At the beginning of your upkeep, if you control five or more Treasures, sacrifice The Rich Fool's Barns and draw two cards.
flavorText: "Soul, thou hast much goods laid up for many years; take thine ease, eat, drink, and be merry." — Luke 12:19
notes: Created from a checked Messianic Fulfillment card list item; first-pass mana, colors, and mechanics were added, but final tuning remains pending. Source boundary is anchored to Luke with Matthew, Mark, and John as support material per the Biblical-series timeline guide. Classics theme art uses Rembrandt's "The Parable of the Rich Fool" via Wikimedia Commons.

classics.license: Public domain; Commons notes the original work is public-domain.
classics.creditText: Rembrandt, The Parable of the Rich Fool
classics.sourceNotes: Best classical fit for the barns-and-futility theme.
